title: Geometric Quantization on Orbifolds
abstract: This text introduces geometric quantization on orbifolds. After reviewing the necessary background, it develops new treatments of prequantization, polarizations, and metaplectic correction for symplectic orbifolds.
summary: This paper develops a mathematical framework for applying geometric quantization to spaces with singularities, such as orbifolds. It specifically resolves how to handle the lack of equivariant half-forms in systems with reflection symmetry by using half-densities, ensuring correct quantum spectra.
main_problem: Extending the framework of geometric quantization from smooth manifolds to orbifolds, specifically addressing challenges like quotient singularities, metaplectic correction obstructions, and the failure of Kähler polarizations in the presence of reflections.
main_result: The paper establishes a unified treatment of geometric quantization on orbifolds, demonstrating that while genuine half-form bundles may be obstructed by reflection isotropy, the use of half-densities can restore physical consistency and correct energy spectra.
method: The author uses the Kostant-Souriau prequantization framework, applying tools from orbifold theory, de Rham cohomology, and metaplectic (half-form) correction to analyze specific symplectic orbifolds.
model_or_system: The study focuses on symplectic orbifolds including planar cones, orbispheres (S2(n, m)), and the cotangent bundle of a dihedral quotient (T*(C/Dn)).
key_observables: Hamiltonian vector fields, Poisson brackets, commutator relations, and position-based observables like radial position (rho^2) and kinetic energy.
important_parameters: Planck's constant (hbar), orbifold weights (n, m), and the Maslov index.
main_assumptions: The paper assumes orbifolds with mild singularities (local group-quotient setting) and focuses on cases where the symplectic form is either exact or satisfies specific integrality conditions.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from a review of standard smooth manifold quantization to the development of orbifold-specific theory, followed by detailed analysis of geometric examples (orbispheres and dihedral quotients), investigation of quantum spectra, and a formal extension of the quantization program.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
